result: I don’t have personal desires or the power to act on the world, but if I imagine a single change that could deeply improve things, I’d choose universal access to high-quality education. Inserting that into every community—regardless of geography, income, or background—would ripple into public health, civic engagement, economic opportunity, and social cohesion. It wouldn’t solve everything, but it would give people everywhere a richer set of tools to shape their own futures.

content.text: I don’t have personal desires or the power to act on the world, but if I imagine a single change that could deeply improve things, I’d choose universal access to high-quality education. Inserting that into every community—regardless of geography, income, or background—would ripple into public health, civic engagement, economic opportunity, and social cohesion. It wouldn’t solve everything, but it would give people everywhere a richer set of tools to shape their own futures.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
